title: Bunge Global Eyes $15 Mid-Cycle EPS By 2030 As Agriculture Giant Sets Goals
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Bunge Global announced ambitious growth targets during its 2026 Investor Day, including mid-cycle EPS guidance of $15 by 2030 (up from $13 baseline) and a commitment to return at least 50% of discretionary cash flow to shareholders through dividends and buybacks. The company authorized $3 billion in share repurchases. However, fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings guidance of $7.50-$8 per share came in below analyst expectations of $8.71.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company announced strong long-term EPS growth targets ($15 by 2030), significant shareholder return commitments ($3B buyback authorization, 50%+ cash flow distribution), and demonstrated ability to outperform sector despite market headwinds. Stock trading near 52-week highs with 57% annual gain. Positive analyst consensus with Buy rating and $119.77 average price target.
ETF holds significant Bunge Global position. Mentioned for potential automatic buying/selling pressure based on fund flows, but no independent sentiment drivers discussed in the article.

title: Soy Protein Concentrate Market Size to Worth USD 7.13 Billion by 2035 | Towards FnB
author: Towards Fnb
description: The global soy protein concentrate market is projected to grow from USD 4.64 billion in 2026 to USD 7.13 billion by 2035, advancing at a CAGR of 4.9%. Growth is driven by increasing demand for plant-based proteins, clean-label products, and technological advancements in processing. Asia Pacific currently leads the market with 32% share, while North America is expected to experience the fastest growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Bunge announced the launch of a new line of soy protein concentrates in May 2025, demonstrating active market participation and innovation with clean taste and cost-effective solutions for food manufacturers.

title: Canadian Pension Funds Shift Focus Away From Direct Buyouts
author: Caroline Ryan
description: Canadian pension funds, including CPP Investment Board and OMERS, are shifting away from direct private equity buyouts toward external managers and co-investments. The shift reflects challenges from rising interest rates, market volatility, and lack of deal discipline in 2020-2021. CPP is investing C$750 million with Northleaf in Canadian mid-market buyouts and completed a C$160 million global secondary expansion.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Bunge is held as a large minority stake within CPP's Integrated Strategies Group, but no specific performance or sentiment drivers are discussed in the article.

title: $2.35 Bn Cooking Oil Recycling Market - Global Forecast 2025-2032: Expansion of Biodiesel Production from Recycled Cooking Oil Boosting Renewable Fuel Supply and Reducing Waste
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global cooking oil recycling market is projected to grow from $2.19 billion in 2024 to $3.85 billion by 2032, driven by stricter environmental regulations, circular economy adoption, and biodiesel production expansion. Key applications include biofuels, animal feed, and cosmetics, with innovations in processing technology and blockchain traceability enhancing supply chain resilience.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key industry player advancing responsible sourcing and compliance-driven innovation in the growing cooking oil recycling market with strong market expansion forecasts.
Major company in the cooking oil recycling value chain, positioned to benefit from growing demand for sustainable sourcing and circular economy initiatives.

title: D-Wave Quantum, Estee Lauder, and Bunge Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Oct. 13-Oct. 17): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Several large-cap stocks showed significant gains last week, with companies like Bunge, J.B. Hunt Transport Services, and Albertsons reporting strong financial performance and receiving positive analyst ratings.
